    • 洛州道光坊 - A district in Luoyang.
    • 逆韦 - A historical figure.
    • 神龙 - A reign title.
    • 突厥 - A historical ethnic group.
    • 圣善寺 - A temple.
    • 景云 - A reign title.
    • 谯王 - A prince.
    • 天枢 - A monument.
    • 阿韦 - A historical figure.

    Proper Nouns

    • 周如意年中 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor Ruyi of Zhou.
    • 黄麞歌 - A song.
    • 契丹 - A historical ethnic group.
    • 都督赵翽 - A high-ranking military official.
    • 曹仁师张遇麻仁节王孝杰 - Names of military leaders.
    • 黄麞谷 - A specific location.
    • 周垂拱 - A reign title.
    • 苾拏儿 - A term used in the song lyrics.
    • 张易之 - A historical figure.
    • 安乐公主 - A princess.
    • 洛州道光坊 - A district in Luoyang.
    • 逆韦 - A historical figure.
    • 神龙 - A reign title.
    • 乌鹊窠 - A metaphor.
    • 金骆駞 - A metaphor.
    • 突厥 - A historical ethnic group.
    • 圣善寺 - A temple.
    • 景云 - A reign title.
    • 谯王 - A prince.
    • 天枢 - A monument.
    • 阿韦 - A historical figure.

    During the year of Emperor Ruyi of Zhou, the Yellow Deer Song first began to be sung. Its lyrics say: "Yellow deer, yellow deer, hidden in the grass; a bow shoots you and you are wounded." Soon after, the Khitan rebelled, killing the governor Zhao Wei, and his camp and office were captured and destroyed. The supreme commander, Cao Renshi, Zhang Xuanyu, Ma Renshi, and Wang Xiaojie, along with hundreds of thousands of troops, were defeated by the rebels in the Yellow Deer Valley, and all the armies were annihilated, leaving no survivors. The Yellow Deer Song thus proved to be true.

    From the reign of Emperor Chui Gong onward, the lyrics of the Bina'er were all evil and crooked. Later, Zhang Yizhi, whose pet name was Bina'er, was a historical figure.

    In the year of Jinglong, Princess Anle built the Anle Temple in the Daoguang Ward of Luoyang, spending several million coins. A children's rhyme said: "Poor Anle Temple, the tree is hung high." Afterward, the rebel Wei was executed, and Princess Anle was also killed, and her head was hung on a pole, and she was posthumously titled a traitorous commoner.

    After the reign of Shenlong, a rhyme said: "South of the mountain, the nests of crows and magpies; north of the mountain, the golden camel. The sickle blade is not sharpened, and the axe is not fitted with a handle." This is the response to the Khitan's strength, where the people could not cut mulberry trees for silkworms, plant trees, or harvest grain.

    In the year of Jinglong, a rhyme said: "Poor Saintly Temple, dressed in green fur, pulled to drink in the river, and the carp was trampled to death." By the time of Jingyun, Prince Chao from Junzhou entered the capital to rebel, but he fled and was killed in Luoyang.

    In the year of Jingyun, a rhyme said: "A single hemp thread pulls the Tianzhu, it breaks away. When Emperor Shenwu ascended the throne, he ordered the Tianzhu to be torn down, and the copper was collected and sent to the imperial treasury." This is the omen.

    In the year of Jinglong, a rhyme said: "The yellow pine ox pulls the harness, the two feet are on the ground, the shoe laces are broken. In June, King Ping executed the rebel Wei, who wanted to rebel. The broken shoe laces mean the rebellion will not succeed. Wei is the descendant of the yellow ox." [12]

    明堂主簿骆宾王帝京篇曰倐忽风生羽翼须臾失浪委泥沙宾王后与徐敬业兴兵州大败投江而死此其防也
    　　麟徳已来百姓饮酒唱歌曲终而不尽者号为族盐后阎知微从突厥领贼破赵定后知微来则天大怒磔于西市命百官射之河内王武懿宗去七步射三发皆不中其怯懦也如此知微身上箭如猬毛剉其骨肉夷其九族疎亲及不相识者皆斩之小儿年七八嵗驱抱向西市百姓哀之掷饼果与者相争夺以为戏笑监刑御史不忍害奏舎之其族盐之言于斯应也
    　　赵公长孙无忌以乌羊毛为浑脱毡帽天下慕之其帽为赵公浑脱后坐事长流岭南浑脱之言于是效焉
    　　魏王为巾子向前踏天下欣欣慕之名为魏王踣后坐死至孝和时陆颂亦为巾子同此様时人又名为陆颂踣未一年而陆颂殒
    　　永徽后天下唱武媚娘歌后立武氏为皇后大帝崩则天临朝改号大周二十余年武后彊盛武三王梁魏定等并开府自余郡王十余人防迁鼎矣 [13]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [13]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 倏忽 - Classical adverb meaning 'suddenly' or 'in a moment,' often used to describe rapid changes.
    • 羽翼 - Literally 'feathers and wings,' used metaphorically to signify power or influence.
    • 須臾 - Adverb meaning 'in a moment' or 'very soon.'
    • 失浪委泥沙 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'lost in the waves and deposited in the mud and sand,' symbolizing a sudden downfall or loss.
    • 興兵 - Means 'to raise an army' or 'to launch a military campaign.'
    • 大败 - Means 'a great defeat' or 'a crushing loss.'
    • 族盐 - Metaphorical term that likely refers to a specific cultural or historical reference, possibly related to a salt-making process or a metaphor for something being broken down.
    • 后知微来 - Refers to the return of Yan Zhiwei, a historical figure.
    • 则天大怒 - Indicates Empress Wu was greatly angered.
    • 磔于西市 - Indicates crucifixion in the Western Market, a form of public execution.
    • 命百官射之 - Indicates ordering officials to shoot him, a cruel and public punishment.
    • 河内王武懿宗 - Refers to Prince Wu Yizong of Hebei, a historical figure.
    • 去七步射三发皆不中 - Describes Prince Wu Yizong taking seven steps, firing three arrows, and all missing, highlighting his cowardice.
    • 怯懦也如此 - Direct statement of the prince's character, indicating his cowardice.
    • 身上箭如猬毛 - Vivid description of the severity of the punishment, with arrows stuck to his body like a hedgehog's spines.
    • 剉其骨肉 - Brutal description of the torture, crushing his bones and flesh.
    • 夷其九族 - Severe punishment extending to family members, eliminating his nine clans.
    • 疎亲及不相识者皆斩之 - Extreme nature of the punishment, even distant relatives and strangers were all executed.
    • 小儿年七八岁 - Emphasizes the cruelty of the punishment, referring to children aged seven or eight years old.
    • 掷饼果与者相争夺以为戏笑 - Highlights the public nature of the cruelty, people throwing food and fruits at them, competing to hit them as a form of entertainment.
    • 监刑御史不忍害奏舍之 - Shows a rare act of mercy, the supervising censor could not bear to harm them and reported to save them.
    • 防也 - Indicates a moral or historical lesson to be drawn, a warning.
    • 赵公长孙无忌 - Refers to Grand Tutor Changsun Wuji, a high-ranking official.
    • 乌羊毛为浑脱毡帽 - Describes a hat made from black sheep wool, a specific type of hat.
    • 天下慕之 - Indicates widespread popularity, the whole world admired it.
    • 其帽为赵公浑脱 - Specific name for the hat, his hat was called the Sogdian Turban.
    • 坐事长流岭南 - Severe punishment, imprisoned and exiled to Lingnan.
    • 浑脱之言 - Indicates the origin of the term, the term 'Sogdian Turban.'
    • 效焉 - Indicates the imitation of the hat, followed suit.
    • 魏王 - Refers to a title given to a member of the imperial family, Prince Wei.
    • 巾子向前踏 - Describes a specific style of headwear, a headscarf worn forward.
    • 天下欣欣慕之 - Indicates widespread popularity, the whole world admired it.
    • 名为魏王踣 - Specific name for the headscarf, called the Prince Wei's Fall.
    • 坐死 - Indicates a severe punishment, died due to a crime.
    • 至孝和时 - Historical reference, by the time of Emperor Xuanzong.
    • 陆颂亦为巾子 - Indicates a similar style, Lu Song also wore a headscarf.
    • 同此様 - Indicates similarity, in the same style.
    • 未一年而陆颂殒 - Indicates a short lifespan, within a year, Lu Song died.
    • 永徽后 - Historical reference, after the Yonghui era.
    • 天下唱武媚娘歌 - Indicates widespread popularity, the whole world sang the song of Wu Meiniang.
    • 后立武氏为皇后 - Significant historical event, later, Wu Zetian was made empress.
    • 大帝崩 - Historical reference, the great emperor died.
    • 则天临朝 - Significant historical event, Empress Wu took power.
    • 改号大周 - Significant historical event, changed the dynasty to Great Zhou.
    • 二十余年 - Indicates a long period, more than twenty years.
    • 武后彊盛 - Indicates her political influence, Empress Wu was strong and powerful.
    • 武三王梁魏定等 - Historical figures, three princes of Wu, including Liang, Wei, and Ding.
    • 并开府 - Indicates political power, all established their own offices.
    • 自余郡王十余人 - Indicates a large number of powerful figures, more than ten other marquises.
    • 防迁鼎矣 - Indicates a significant political change, the transfer of power was imminent.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 骆宾王 - Famous poet and statesman of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 徐敬业 - Tang general who rebelled against the court.
    • 扬州市 - City in present-day Jiangsu Province.
    • 阎知微 - Tang official who defected to the Turks.
    • 突厥 - Nomadic confederation that dominated Central Asia.
    • 赵定 - Place name, likely a city or region.
    • 则天 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
    • 西市 - Market in Chang'an (modern Xi'an), often used for public executions.
    • 长孙无忌 - High-ranking official and grand tutor during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 浑脱毡帽 - Type of hat made from black sheep wool, popular in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 魏王 - Title given to a member of the imperial family.
    • 巾子 - Type of headscarf or cap.
    • 武媚娘 - Nickname for Empress Wu Zetian, meaning 'beautiful and charming.'
    • 大周 - Dynasty established by Empress Wu Zetian.
    • 武三王梁魏定等 - High-ranking officials and princes during the Tang Dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 骆宾王 - Famous poet and statesman of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 徐敬业 - Tang general who rebelled against the court.
    • 扬州市 - City in present-day Jiangsu Province.
    • 阎知微 - Tang official who defected to the Turks.
    • 突厥 - Nomadic confederation that dominated Central Asia.
    • 赵定 - Place name, likely a city or region.
    • 则天 - Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
    • 西市 - Market in Chang'an (modern Xi'an), often used for public executions.
    • 长孙无忌 - High-ranking official and grand tutor during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 浑脱毡帽 - Type of hat made from black sheep wool, popular in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 魏王 - Title given to a member of the imperial family.
    • 巾子 - Type of headscarf or cap.
    • 武媚娘 - Nickname for Empress Wu Zetian, meaning 'beautiful and charming.'
    • 大周 - Dynasty established by Empress Wu Zetian.
    • 武三王梁魏定等 - High-ranking officials and princes during the Tang Dynasty.

    The Chief Clerk of the Hall of Brightness, Lu Binwang, wrote in his Capital City Ode: "Suddenly, a wind rises, and wings are born; in an instant, lost in the waves and deposited in the mud and sand." Afterward, Lu Binwang joined Xu Jingye in raising an army in Yangzhou, suffered a great defeat, and drowned in the river, which was a warning to others.

    Since the Lintai era, when people drank and sang songs, those who continued singing after the music ended were called "salted fish." Later, Yan Zhiwei, who defected to the Turks, led them to conquer Zhao Ding. After Yan Zhiwei returned, Empress Wu was greatly angered and crucified him in the Western Market, ordering officials to shoot him. Prince Wu Yizong of Hebei took seven steps, fired three arrows, and all missed, revealing his cowardice. Yan Zhiwei's body was riddled with arrows like a hedgehog's spines, and his bones and flesh were crushed. His nine clans were eliminated, and even distant relatives and strangers were all executed. Children aged seven or eight were forced to watch, and people threw food and fruits at them, competing to hit them as a form of entertainment. The supervising censor could not bear to harm them and reported to save them. This is how the term "salted fish" came to be.

    Grand Tutor Changsun Wuji made a hat from black sheep wool, and the whole world admired it. His hat was called the "Sogdian Turban." Later, he was imprisoned and exiled to Lingnan for his crimes. The term "Sogdian Turban" was thus adopted.

    Prince Wei wore a headscarf worn forward, and the whole world admired it. It was called the "Prince Wei's Fall." After his death due to a crime, by the time of Emperor Xuanzong, Lu Song also wore a headscarf in the same style, and it was called the "Lu Song's Fall." Within a year, Lu Song died.

    After the Yonghui era, the whole world sang the song of Wu Meiniang. Later, Wu Zetian was made empress. The great emperor died, and Empress Wu took power, changing the dynasty to Great Zhou. For more than twenty years, Empress Wu was strong and powerful. The Three Princes of Wu, including Liang, Wei, and Ding, all established their own offices, and more than ten other marquises joined them. The transfer of power was imminent. [13]

    咸亨已后人皆云莫浪语阿婆嗔三叔闻时笑杀人后果则天即位至孝和嗣之阿婆者则天也三叔者孝和为第三也
    　　魏仆射子名叔麟防者曰叔麟反语身戮也后果被罗织而诛
    　　梁王武三思唐神龙初改封徳靖王防者言徳靖鼎贼也果有窥鼎之志被郑克等斩之
    　　天后时謡言曰张公吃酒李公醉张公者斥易之兄弟也李公者言李氏大盛也
    　　孙佺为幽州都督五月北征时军师李处郁谏五月南方火北方水火入水必灭佺不从果没八万人昔窦建徳救王世充于牛口谷时谓窦入牛口岂有还期果被秦王所擒其孙佺之北也处郁曰飧若入咽百无一全山东人谓温饭为飱幽州以北并为燕地故云
    　　龙朔年已来百姓饮酒作令云子母相去离连台拗倒子母者盏与盘也连台者连盘拗倒盏也及天后永昌中罗织事起有宿卫十余人于清化坊饮为此令此席人进状告之十人皆弃市自后庐陵徙均州则子母相去离也连台拗倒者则天被废诸武迁放之兆 [14]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [14]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 莫浪语 - Admonitory expression meaning 'do not speak carelessly' or 'do not say things without thought.'
    • 阿婆嗔三叔 - Colloquial phrase where '阿婆' (Apo) refers to Empress Wu Zetian, and '三叔' (San Shu) refers to Emperor Ruizong, the third son of Emperor Gaozong.
    • 笑杀人 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'laughed to death' or 'killed by laughter.'
    • 后果 - Means 'as a result' or 'consequently.'
    • 鼎贼 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'those who covet the throne' or 'rebellious usurpers.'
    • 被郑克等斩之 - Means 'were killed by Zheng Ke and others.'
    • 子母相去离 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'mother and child are separated.'
    • 连台拗倒 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the platform collapses.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 咸亨 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Tang (670-674 AD).
    • 则天 - The temple name of Empress Wu Zetian, who ruled as empress regnant and later as emperor.
    • 孝和 - The posthumous name of Emperor Ruizong, who was the third son of Emperor Gaozong.
    • 魏仆射子名叔麟 - Refers to Wei Shuyin, a high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
    • 梁王武三思 - Refers to Wu Sansi, a powerful nobleman during the Tang dynasty.
    • 神龙初 - The beginning of the Shenlong era (705-707 AD), when Empress Wu was overthrown and Emperor Zhongzong restored.
    • 罗织 - A term for the fabrication of crimes, often used to describe the political purges during Empress Wu's reign.
    • 宿卫 - Refers to palace guards or court officials.
    • 清化坊 - A district in Chang'an, the capital of the Tang dynasty.
    • 庐陵 - A place name, likely referring to the original residence of the Tang imperial family.
    • 均州 - A place name, likely referring to the location of exile.
    • 子母 - Refers to a mother and child, often used metaphorically in this context.
    • 连台 - Refers to a platform or stage, often used metaphorically.
    • 拗倒 - Means 'to collapse' or 'to fall over.'
    • 被废 - Means 'to be deposed' or 'to be removed from power.'
    • 诸武迁放 - Refers to the exiling of various members of the Wu family.

    Proper Nouns

    • 咸亨 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Tang.
    • 则天 - The temple name of Empress Wu Zetian.
    • 孝和 - The posthumous name of Emperor Ruizong.
    • 魏仆射子名叔麟 - Wei Shuyin, a high-ranking official.
    • 梁王武三思 - Wu Sansi, a powerful nobleman.
    • 神龙 - The era name of the early reign of Emperor Zhongzong.
    • 徳靖 - The title given to Wu Sansi.
    • 张公 - A metaphor for the powerful figures of the time.
    • 李公 - A metaphor for the powerful figures of the time.
    • 孙佺 - A military general.
    • 李处郁 - A military advisor.
    • 窦建徳 - A rebel leader.
    • 王世充 - A rival warlord.
    • 秦王 - A title, likely referring to Emperor Taizong.
    • 龙朔 - The era name of Emperor Gaozong of Tang.
    • 庐陵 - A place name.
    • 均州 - A place name.
    • 清化坊 - A district in Chang'an.
    • 郑克 - A person involved in the political purges.
    • 诸武 - Various members of the Wu family.

    After the Xianheng era, people all said, "Do not speak carelessly, or the old lady will be angry with the third uncle." When the third uncle heard it, he laughed until he died. As a result, Empress Wu ascended the throne, and until Emperor Ruizong succeeded him, the old lady referred to was Empress Wu, and the third uncle referred to was Ruizong, the third son of Emperor Gaozong.

    Wei Shuyin, the son of the Chief Secretary, named Shuyin. The name "Shuyin" reversed reads as "execution." Consequently, he was accused and executed.

    Wu Sansi, the Prince of Liang, was given the title of Duke of Dejing at the beginning of the Shenlong era. The title "Dejing" was a metaphor for those who coveted the throne. He indeed had ambitions to seize the throne and was killed by Zheng Ke and others.

    During the reign of Empress Wu, there was a saying: "Zhang Gong drinks wine, Li Gong is drunk." "Zhang Gong" referred to the brothers of Zhang Yi, and "Li Gong" referred to the prosperity of the Li family.

    Sun Quan, the Governor of Youzhou, led a northern campaign in May. His military advisor, Li Chuyu, advised him, "In May, the south is fire and the north is water. Fire entering water will surely be extinguished." Sun Quan did not listen, and as a result, eight thousand men were lost. In the past, when Dou Jiande rescued Wang Shichong at Niukou Valley, he said, "Dou entering Niukou, how could there be a return?" Indeed, he was captured by Emperor Taizong. When Sun Quan went north, Li Chuyu said, "If food enters the throat, there is no hope of survival." People in Shandong called warm rice "su," and Youzhou and beyond were all part of Yan territory, hence the saying.

    Since the Longshuo era, people have made a drinking game: "Mother and child are separated, the platform collapses." "Mother and child" referred to the cup and plate, and "platform" referred to the plate collapsing and the cup falling over. During the Yongchang era of Empress Wu, the fabrication of crimes began. Ten palace guards were drinking in Qinghua Ward and played this game. One of them reported it, and all ten were executed in public. Later, when the original residence of the imperial family was exiled to Junzhou, "mother and child are separated" referred to the separation of the imperial family, and "platform collapses" referred to Empress Wu being deposed and the Wu family being exiled. [14]

    神武皇帝七月即位东都白马寺铁像头无故自落于殿门外自后捉搦僧尼严急令拜父母等未成者并停革后出者科决还俗者十八九焉
    　　开元五年春司天奏象有眚见其灾甚重宗震惊问曰何祲对曰当有名士三十人同日寃死今新及第进士正应其数其年及第李者贵主家婿上不言其事宻戒主曰每有大游宴汝爱婿可闲留其家主居昭国里时大合乐音曲逺畅曲江涨水聨舟数艘进士毕集闻乃逾垣奔走羣众惬望才登舟移就水中画舸平沉声妓篙工不知纪极三十进士无一生者
    　　夏侯处信为荆州长史有宾过之处信命仆作食仆附耳语曰溲防许麺信曰两人二升即可矣仆入久不出宾以事告去信遽呼仆仆曰已溲讫信鸣指曰大异事良久乃曰可总燔作饼吾公退食之信又尝以一小瓶贮醯一升自食家人不霑余沥仆云醋尽信取瓶合于掌上余数滴因以口吸之乃授直去凡市易必经手识者鄙之 [15]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [15]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 神武皇帝 - Title for Emperor Taizong of Tang, indicating his military prowess and divine mandate.
    • 即位 - Means 'ascended the throne.'
    • 东都 - Refers to Luoyang, the eastern capital of the Tang dynasty.
    • 白马寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Luoyang, one of the earliest in China.
    • 铁像头无故自落 - Literal description of a statue's head falling off without apparent cause, likely interpreted as an omen.
    • 捉搦僧尼严急 - Means 'strictly enforced restrictions on monks and nuns.'
    • 拜父母 - Refers to the practice of monks and nuns showing respect to their parents.
    • 科决 - Means 'punishment' or 'legal penalty.'
    • 还俗者十八九焉 - Indicates that about 80-90% of those affected returned to secular life.
    • 司天 - Refers to the official responsible for astronomy and astrology.
    • 玄象有眚 - Means 'anomalies in the celestial phenomena,' often interpreted as bad omens.
    • 见其灾甚重 - Indicates that the disaster was severe.
    • 开元五年 - The fifth year of the Kaiyuan era during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
    • 及第 - Means 'passed the imperial examinations,' referring to successful candidates.
    • 贵主家婿 - Refers to a wealthy nobleman's son-in-law.
    • 大游宴 - Means 'grand banquets' or 'large-scale feasts.'
    • 昭国里 - A specific area in Luoyang.
    • 合乐音曲 - Means 'music and songs performed together.'
    • 曲江涨水 - Refers to the flooding of the Qujiang Pool, a famous scenic spot in Luoyang.
    • 连舟数艘 - Means 'several connected boats.'
    • 逾垣奔走 - Means 'jumping over walls and running away.'
    • 惬望 - Means 'satisfied' or 'pleased.'
    • 画舸平沉 - Means 'the painted boats sank levelly.'
    • 声妓篙工 - Refers to musicians, singers, and boatmen.
    • 不知纪极 - Means 'without end' or 'unlimited.'
    • 三十进士无一生者 - Indicates that all thirty successful candidates died.
    • 夏侯处信 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official.
    • 荆州长史 - A high-ranking official in charge of the prefecture of Jingzhou.
    • 宾过之处 - Means 'when a guest visited.'
    • 溲面 - Means 'kneading dough.'
    • 附耳语 - Means 'whispering in the ear.'
    • 两人二升即可矣 - Means 'two people with two sheng each will suffice.'
    • 已溲讫 - Means 'already kneaded.'
    • 大异事 - Means 'a strange event.'
    • 可总燔作饼 - Means 'can be burned and made into cakes.'
    • 公退食之 - Means 'eat after official duties.'
    • 贮醯 - Means 'store vinegar.'
    • 不霑余沥 - Means 'no leftover drops.'
    • 醋尽 - Means 'the vinegar is finished.'
    • 取瓶合于掌上 - Means 'took the bottle and placed it in his hand.'
    • 余数滴 - Means 'a few drops remaining.'
    • 以口吸之 - Means 'sucked it with his mouth.'
    • 授直去 - Means 'gave the price and left.'
    • 市易 - Means 'market transactions.'
    • 识者鄙之 - Means 'people who know about it look down on him.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 神武皇帝 - Emperor Taizong of Tang, known for his military conquests and political reforms.
    • 开元五年 - The fifth year of the Kaiyuan era (717 AD) during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
    • 白马寺 - One of the oldest Buddhist temples in China, founded in 68 AD.
    • 玄象有眚 - Refers to astrological anomalies that were often interpreted as omens of disaster.
    • 贵主家婿 - A wealthy nobleman's son-in-law, indicating social status.
    • 大游宴 - Grand banquets were common in imperial China, often involving music, dance, and poetry.
    • 曲江涨水 - The Qujiang Pool was a famous scenic area in Luoyang, known for its beauty and cultural significance.
    • 夏侯处信 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Tang dynasty.
    • 荆州 - A prefecture in southern China, historically significant for its strategic location.

    Proper Nouns

    • 神武皇帝 - Emperor Taizong of Tang.
    • 东都 - Luoyang.
    • 白马寺 - White Horse Temple.
    • 开元五年 - The fifth year of the Kaiyuan era (717 AD).
    • 夏侯处信 - A historical figure.
    • 荆州 - A prefecture in southern China.

    Translation:

    In the seventh month, Emperor Taizong ascended the throne in the eastern capital, Luoyang. At the White Horse Temple, the head of a bronze statue fell off the gate of the hall without apparent cause. After this, strict laws were enforced against monks and nuns, compelling them to show respect to their parents. Those who had not completed this ritual were dismissed, and those who left after being dismissed were punished. About eighty to ninety percent of them returned to secular life.

    In the fifth year of the Kaiyuan era, in spring, the official responsible for astronomy reported that there were anomalies in the celestial phenomena, indicating a severe disaster. Emperor Xuanzong was shocked and asked, "What is this omen?" The official replied, "There should be thirty distinguished scholars who will die unjustly on the same day. This year, the new successful candidates in the imperial examinations match this number." One of these successful candidates, Li, was the son-in-law of a wealthy nobleman. The emperor did not mention this matter but secretly warned the nobleman, saying, "Whenever there is a grand banquet, you may keep your son-in-law at home." The nobleman lived in Zhaoguo Lane, where music and songs were performed, and the Qujiang Pool was flooded, with several connected boats gathering all the successful candidates. Li heard about it and jumped over the wall to run away. The crowd was pleased, and he quickly boarded the boat, which then moved into the water, sinking levelly. The musicians, singers, and boatmen continued endlessly, and all thirty successful candidates died.

    Shah Hou Chuxin was the governor of Jingzhou. When a guest visited, he ordered his servant to prepare food. The servant whispered in his ear, saying, "We need to knead dough for the bread." Shah Hou replied, "Two people with two sheng each will suffice." The servant entered the kitchen but did not come out for a long time. The guest reported this to Shah Hou, who hurriedly called the servant. The servant said, "I have already kneaded the dough." Shah Hou exclaimed, "A strange event has occurred!" After a while, he said, "We can burn the dough and make it into cakes. I will eat them after my official duties." Shah Hou also once stored a small bottle of vinegar, one sheng, for himself. His family did not get any leftovers. The servant said the vinegar was finished. Shah Hou took the bottle and placed it in his hand, noticing a few drops remaining, and sucked them with his mouth before giving the price and leaving. In all market transactions, he was involved personally, and those who knew about it looked down on him. [15]

    广州録事参军栁庆独居一室器用食物并致卧内奴有私取盐一撮者庆鞭之见血
    　　夏侯彪夏月食饮生虫在下未曽沥口尝送客出门奴盗食脔肉及彪还觉之大怒乃捉蝇与食令呕出之
    　　郑仁凯为密州刺史有小奴告以履穿凯曰阿翁为汝经营鞋有顷门夫着鞋者至凯防前树上有防窠防啄木也遣门夫上树取其子门夫脱鞋而縁之凯令奴着鞋而去门夫竟至徒跣凯有徳色
    　　安南都防邓祐韶州人家巨富奴婢千人恒课口腹自供未曾设客孙子将一鸭私用祐以擅破家资鞭二十
    　　韦庄颇读书数米而炊秤薪而防炙少一脔即觉之一子八嵗而卒妻敛以时服庄剥取以故席裹尸殡讫擎其席而归其忆念也呜咽不自胜惟悭吝耳 [16]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [16]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 独居一室 - Literally means 'to live alone in one room,' indicating a solitary lifestyle.
    • 器用食物并致卧内 - Means 'all utensils and food are brought into the bedroom,' suggesting a highly private and self-contained living arrangement.
    • 鞭之见血 - Means 'whipped until blood was drawn,' indicating severe punishment.
    • 夏侯彪夏月食饮生虫在下未尝沥口 - Can be interpreted as 'in the summer, when eating and drinking, he would find worms in his food but never bothered to filter them out.'
    • 送客出门奴盗食脔肉 - Means 'when sending guests out, a servant stole a piece of meat.'
    • 鞭二十 - Means 'whipped twenty times,' indicating a standard punishment.
    • 郑仁凯为密州刺史 - Means 'Zheng Rengai served as the governor of Mì Prefecture.'
    • 孙子将一鸭私用 - Means 'his grandson took a duck for personal use.'
    • 擅破家资 - Means 'misappropriated family assets,' indicating unauthorized use of family property.
    • 剥取以故席裹尸殡讫擎其席而归 - Can be interpreted as 'he stripped off the old mat to wrap the body, after which he carried the mat back home.'
    • 忆念也呜咽不自胜 - Means 'he was overwhelmed with grief and wept uncontrollably,' indicating deep emotional distress.
    • 惟悭吝耳 - Means 'only because he was miserly,' suggesting that the action was motivated by greed.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 广州録事参军 - Refers to a lower-ranking official in the Guangzhou prefecture.
    • 密州刺史 - Refers to the governor of Mì Prefecture.
    • 安南都防 - Refers to a military commander in the southern region of China.
    • 邓祐韶州人 - Refers to Deng You, a person from Sháo Prefecture.
    • 韦庄 - Refers to Wei Zhuang, a famous poet and statesman of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 时服 - Refers to the standard clothing of the time, indicating a formal burial.
    • 故席 - Refers to an old mat, which was used to wrap the body.
    • 饭牛牧羊 - Refers to feeding cattle and herding sheep, indicating a simple and humble lifestyle.

    Guangzhou Luqu Shangjun Liu Qing lived alone in one room, bringing all his utensils and food into his bedroom. When a servant privately took a pinch of salt, Liu Qing whipped him until blood was drawn.

    During the summer, Xiahoubiao would find worms in his food and drinks but never bothered to filter them out. Once, when he was sending guests out, a servant stole a piece of meat. When Xiahoubiao returned and discovered this, he became furious and caught a fly, fed it the meat, and made the servant vomit it out.

    Zheng Rengai served as the governor of Mì Prefecture. One day, a young servant reported that his shoes were worn out. Zheng said, "Grandfather will take care of it for you." Soon, a man with shoes arrived. Zheng ordered a servant to go up a tree and retrieve some woodpecker eggs. The man had to remove his shoes to climb the tree. Zheng then ordered the servant to put on the shoes and leave. The man finally arrived barefoot, and Zheng looked pleased.

    Deng You, the military commander of the southern region, was a wealthy man from Sháo Prefecture with a thousand servants and slaves. He always provided for their daily needs but never entertained guests. One day, his grandson took a duck for personal use. Deng punished him for misappropriating family assets, whipping him twenty times.

    Wei Zhuang, a well-read man, measured his rice before cooking and weighed his firewood before burning it. If even a small piece of meat was missing, he would notice. His son, who was eight years old, died. His wife wrapped the body in the standard clothing of the time, and Wei Zhuang stripped off the old mat to wrap the body. After the burial, he carried the mat back home, overwhelmed with grief and wept uncontrollably. It was only because he was miserly. [16]

    怀州录事参军路敬潜遭綦连辉事于新开推鞫免死配流后诉雪授睦州遂安县令前邑宰皆卒于官潜欲不赴其妻曰君若合死新开之难早已无身今得县令岂非命乎遂至州去县水路数百里上寝堂两间有三殡坑皆埋旧县令潜命坊夫填之有枭鸣于屛风又鸣于承尘上并不以为事每与妻对食有鼠数十头或黄或白或青或黑以杖驱之则抱杖而叫自余妖怪不可具言至四考满一无所失选授卫令除卫州司马入为郎中位至中书舎人
    　　周甘子布博学有才年十七为左卫长史不入五品登封年病以驴轝彊至岳下天恩加两阶合入五品竟不能起乡里亲戚来贺衣冠不得遂以绯覆其上帖然而终
    　　太常卿卢崇道坐女壻中书令崔湜反羽林郎将张仙坐与薛介然口陈欲反之状俱流岭南经年无日不悲号两目皆肿不胜凄楚遂并逃归崇道至都宅藏为男娶崔氏女未成有内给使来取充贵人崇道乃赂给使别取一崔氏女去入内事败给使具承掩崇道幷男三人亦被糺捉敕杖各决一百俱至丧命 [17]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [17]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 遭綦连辉事 - Being involved in a matter related to 'Qílián Huī,' possibly a person or an event.
    • 新开推鞫 - An investigation or trial conducted in a place called 'Xīnkāi.'
    • 免死配流 - Spared from death and exiled.
    • 诉雪 - To appeal and clear one's name.
    • 授睦州遂安县令 - Appointed as the magistrate of Suian County in Muzhou.
    • 前邑宰皆卒于官 - All previous magistrates of the county died in office.
    • 欲不赴 - To want to refuse the appointment.
    • 岂非命乎 - Isn't it fate?
    • 去县水路数百里 - The distance to the county by water is several hundred miles.
    • 上寝堂两间有三殡坑 - In the upper sleeping hall, there are three burial pits.
    • 填之 - To fill them.
    • 枭鸣于屛风又鸣于承尘上 - An owl hooted on the screen and then on the ceiling.
    • 并不以为事 - He did not consider these things to be significant.
    • 每与妻对食 - Every time he ate with his wife.
    • 鼠数十头或黄或白或青或黑 - Dozens of rats, some yellow, some white, some blue, some black.
    • 以杖驱之则抱杖而叫 - When he tried to drive them away with a stick, they would grab the stick and cry.
    • 一无所失 - Without any loss.
    • 选授卫令除卫州司马入为郎中位至中书舎人 - He was selected and appointed as the commandant of Wei, then as the military governor of Weizhou, and later became a Langzhong and eventually a Vice-Chancellor.
    • 经年无日不悲号 - For years, he mourned every day.
    • 两目皆肿 - Both eyes were swollen.
    • 不胜凄楚 - Unable to bear the sorrow.
    • 遂并逃归 - They both fled back.
    • 藏为男娶崔氏女 - He hid and arranged for his son to marry a Cui family daughter.
    • 未成有内给使来取充贵人 - Before it was completed, an imperial messenger came to take her as a concubine.
    • 给使具承掩 - The messenger fully admitted and concealed it.
    • 崇道幷男三人亦被糺捉 - Lu Chongdao and his three sons were also arrested.
    • 敕杖各决一百俱至丧命 - They were each beaten with one hundred strokes and all died.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 綦连辉 - A person or event whose exact nature is unclear without additional context.
    • 新开 - A place where the investigation or trial took place.
    • 配流 - A form of punishment involving exile.
    • 诉雪 - The process of appealing and clearing one's name.
    • 睦州遂安县 - A specific location in Muzhou Prefecture, Zhejiang Province.
    • 上寝堂 - A specific room in a house, likely a bedroom.
    • 枭 - An owl, often associated with omens in Chinese culture.
    • 屛风 - A screen or partition used in traditional Chinese architecture.
    • 承尘 - The ceiling, often made of wood or bamboo in traditional Chinese architecture.
    • 鼠 - Rats, often associated with bad luck or supernatural phenomena in Chinese culture.
    • 郎中 - A mid-level official in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 中书舎人 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 周甘子布 - A historical figure known for his scholarly and intellectual abilities.
    • 登封 - A year in the Chinese calendar, possibly referring to a specific period of political or social significance.
    • 岳 - A mountain, likely a significant religious or cultural site.
    • 太常卿 - The Grand Master of Ceremonies, a high-ranking official in charge of rituals and ceremonies.
    • 中书令 - The Chief Secretary, a high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 羽林郎将 - A military officer in charge of the imperial guard.
    • 薛介然 - A historical figure involved in a political crime.
    • 岭南 - The Lingnan region, a southern part of China known for its tropical climate and diverse culture.

    Proper Nouns

    • 綦连辉 - A person or event whose exact nature is unclear without additional context.
    • 新开 - A place where the investigation or trial took place.
    • 配流 - A form of punishment involving exile.
    • 诉雪 - The process of appealing and clearing one's name.
    • 睦州遂安县 - A specific location in Muzhou Prefecture, Zhejiang Province.
    • 上寝堂 - A specific room in a house, likely a bedroom.
    • 枭 - An owl, often associated with omens in Chinese culture.
    • 屛风 - A screen or partition used in traditional Chinese architecture.
    • 承尘 - The ceiling, often made of wood or bamboo in traditional Chinese architecture.
    • 鼠 - Rats, often associated with bad luck or supernatural phenomena in Chinese culture.
    • 郎中 - A mid-level official in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 中书舎人 - A high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 周甘子布 - A historical figure known for his scholarly and intellectual abilities.
    • 登封 - A year in the Chinese calendar, possibly referring to a specific period of political or social significance.
    • 岳 - A mountain, likely a significant religious or cultural site.
    • 太常卿 - The Grand Master of Ceremonies, a high-ranking official in charge of rituals and ceremonies.
    • 中书令 - The Chief Secretary, a high-ranking official in the imperial bureaucracy.
    • 羽林郎将 - A military officer in charge of the imperial guard.
    • 薛介然 - A historical figure involved in a political crime.
    • 岭南 - The Lingnan region, a southern part of China known for its tropical climate and diverse culture.

    Translation:

    Lu Jingqian, the Clerk of the Court in Huizhou, was involved in an incident related to Qi Lianhui in Xinkai, where he was investigated and spared from death but exiled. After appealing and clearing his name, he was appointed as the magistrate of Suian County in Muzhou. All previous magistrates of the county had died in office, and Lu wanted to refuse the appointment. His wife said, "If you were meant to die, you would have perished in the difficulties of Xinkai long ago. Now that you have become a county magistrate, isn't it fate?" So he went to the state, where the distance to the county by water was several hundred miles. In the upper sleeping hall, there were three burial pits, all containing the remains of former county magistrates. Lu ordered the workers to fill them. An owl hooted on the screen and then on the ceiling, but he did not consider these things to be significant. Every time he ate with his wife, dozens of rats, some yellow, some white, some blue, some black, would appear. When he tried to drive them away with a stick, they would grab the stick and cry. Other supernatural occurrences were too numerous to mention. By the end of his four-year term, he had fulfilled his duties without any loss. He was later selected and appointed as the commandant of Wei, then as the military governor of Weizhou, and eventually became a Langzhong and Vice-Chancellor.

    Zhou Gan Zibu was well-learned and talented. At the age of seventeen, he became the Left Guard Historian but did not reach the fifth rank. In the year of Dengfeng, he fell ill and was carried by a donkey to the foot of a mountain. By imperial grace, he was granted two ranks, bringing him to the fifth rank, but he could not get up. Villagers and relatives came to congratulate him, but he could not wear formal attire. He was covered with a red robe and died calmly.

    Grand Master of Ceremonies Lu Chongdao was exiled to Lingnan for his son-in-law, Chief Secretary Cui Shi, who rebelled; and Yu Lin Langjiang Zhang Xian was exiled for verbally expressing intentions to rebel with Xue Jieren. For years, he mourned every day, and his eyes were swollen. Unable to bear the sorrow, they both fled back. Lu Chongdao hid and arranged for his son to marry a Cui family daughter, but before it was completed, an imperial messenger came to take her as a concubine. The messenger fully admitted and concealed it, and Lu Chongdao and his three sons were also arrested. They were each beaten with one hundred strokes and all died. [17]

    青州刺史刘仁轨知海运失船极多除名为民遂辽东效力遇病卧平壤城下搴幕看兵士攻城有一卒直来前头背坐叱之不去仍恶骂曰你欲看我亦欲看何预汝事不肯去须臾城头放箭正中心而死微此兵仁轨防为矢所中
    　　任之选与张说同时应举后説为中书令之选竟不及第来谒张公公遗绢一束以充粮用之选将归至舎不经一两月疾大作将绢市药绢尽疾自损非但此度余处亦然何薄命之甚也
    　　杭州刺史裴有敞疾甚令钱塘县主簿夏荣看之荣曰使君百无一虑夫人早须崇福以禳之崔夫人曰禳须何物荣曰使君娶二姬以压之出三年则危过矣夫人怒曰此獠狂语儿在身无病荣退曰夫人不信荣不敢言使君命合有三妇不更娶于夫人不祥夫人曰乍可死此事不相当也其年夫人暴亡敞更娶二姓荣言信矣
    　　广平王诛逆韦崔日用将兵杜曲诛诸韦略尽绷子中婴孩亦捏杀之诸杜滥及者非一浮休子曰此逆韦之罪疎族何辜亦如冉闵杀畨高鼻者横死董卓诛阉人无须者枉戮死生命也 [18]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [18]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 知海运失船极多除名为民遂辽东效力 - Knowing about many ship losses, being demoted to commoner status, and subsequently serving in Liaodong to redeem oneself.
    • 遇病卧平壤城下搴幕看兵士攻城 - Encountering illness while lying outside the city walls of Pingyang, pulling aside the curtain to watch soldiers attacking the city.
    • 直来前头背坐叱之不去仍恶骂曰 - Coming straight up to the front, sitting with one's back, scolding and not leaving, still cursing and saying.
    • 你欲看我亦欲看何预汝事 - You want to look, I want to look, what business is it of yours? A defensive and dismissive response.
    • 不肯去须臾城头放箭正中心而死 - Refused to leave, shortly after an arrow was shot from the city wall, hitting him directly in the heart and killing him.
    • 微此兵仁轨防为矢所中 - Without this soldier, Governor Ren Gui would have been hit by an arrow, indicating a narrow escape.
    • 任之选与张说同时应举后说为中书令之选竟不及第 - Ren Zhi was selected to take the imperial examinations at the same time as Zhang Yue, after Zhang Yue became a Grand Secretary, Ren Zhi was never selected.
    • 来谒张公公遗绢一束以充粮用 - Came to visit Zhang Gong, who gave him a bundle of silk to use for food expenses.
    • 将归至舎不经一两月疾大作 - On the way back to his residence, after not even one or two months, his illness worsened.
    • 将绢市药绢尽疾自损 - Used the silk to buy medicine, and when the silk ran out, his illness worsened on its own.
    • 何薄命之甚也 - How unfortunate is my fate! A lamentation about one's misfortune.
    • 杭州刺史裴有敞疾甚令钱塘县主簿夏荣看之 - Governor Pei Youchang of Hangzhou fell gravely ill, ordered the County Magistrate of Qiantang, Xia Rong, to visit him.
    • 荣曰使君百无一虑夫人早须崇福以禳之 - Xia Rong said, 'Sir, there is nothing to worry about; your wife should build merit early to ward off misfortune.'
    • 崔夫人曰禳须何物 - Lady Cui said, 'What is needed to ward off misfortune?'
    • 荣曰使君娶二姬以压之出三年则危过矣 - Xia Rong said, 'Sir, you should marry two concubines to suppress it; if you do not do so within three years, the danger will be even greater.'
    • 夫人怒曰此獠狂语儿在身无病 - Lady Cui angrily said, 'This barbarian is crazy; my son is healthy.'
    • 荣退曰夫人不信荣不敢言使君命合有三妇不更娶于夫人不祥 - Xia Rong stepped back and said, 'Lady, if you do not believe me, I dare not speak further; Sir's fate is destined to have three wives; if he does not marry again, it will be a bad omen.'
    • 夫人曰乍可死此事不相当也 - Lady Cui said, 'It is better to die than to be involved in such matters.'
    • 其年夫人暴亡 - That year, Lady Cui suddenly died.
    • 敞更娶二姓 - Pei Youchang remarried two more women.
    • 荣言信矣 - Xia Rong's words proved true.
    • 广平王诛逆韦崔日用将兵杜曲诛诸韦略尽 - Prince Guangping killed the traitorous Wei Cui, and General Du Qu led the troops to almost completely eliminate the Wei clan.
    • 绷子中婴孩亦捏杀之 - Even infants in their cradles were strangled to death.
    • 诸杜滥及者非一 - Many others were also wrongfully killed.
    • 浮休子曰此逆韦之罪疎族何辜亦如冉闵杀畨高鼻者横死董卓诛阉人无须者枉戮死生命也 - Fuhuizi said, 'This is the crime of the traitorous Wei; why should distant relatives suffer? It is like Ran Min killing foreigners with high noses, or Dong Zhuo executing eunuchs without beards, all of which are unjust deaths; such is the will of fate.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 青州 - A region in northern China, historically significant.
    • 辽东 - A region in northeastern China, often associated with military campaigns.
    • 平壤 - A city in Korea, historically part of Chinese territory.
    • 张说 - A prominent statesman and poet during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 杭州 - A city in eastern China, known for its cultural and economic importance.
    • 钱塘县 - A county in Hangzhou, historically significant.
    • 广平王 - A title for a prince during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 逆韦 - Refers to Wei Cui, a traitor during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 崔日用 - A military general during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 杜曲 - A place name, possibly referring to a strategic location.
    • 浮休子 - A pseudonym or nickname, possibly a scholar or philosopher.

    Proper Nouns

    • 青州刺史 - Title meaning 'Governor of Qingzhou,' indicating a high-ranking official.
    • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.
    • 杭州刺史裴有敞 - Governor Pei Youchang of Hangzhou.
    • 钱塘县主簿夏荣 - County Magistrate of Qiantang, Xia Rong.
    • 崔夫人 - Lady Cui.
    • 张公 - Zhang Gong.
    • 广平王 - Prince Guangping.
    • 逆韦 - Wei Cui, a traitor during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 崔日用 - Military general during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 杜曲 - A place name, possibly referring to a strategic location.
    • 浮休子 - Pseudonym or nickname, possibly a scholar or philosopher.

    Governor Liu Rengui of Qingzhou, upon learning of the many ships lost during maritime transport, was stripped of his title and demoted to commoner status. Subsequently, he served in Liaodong to redeem himself. While lying outside the walls of Pingyang, he fell ill and pulled aside the curtain to watch soldiers attacking the city. Suddenly, a soldier came straight up to the front and sat with his back to him, scolding him and not leaving. The soldier continued to curse, saying, "You want to look, I want to look, what business is it of yours?" Refusing to leave, the soldier was soon hit by an arrow from the city wall, which struck him directly in the heart and killed him. Without this soldier, Governor Liu Rengui would have been hit by the arrow.

    Ren Zhi was selected to take the imperial examinations at the same time as Zhang Yue. After Zhang Yue became a Grand Secretary, Ren Zhi was never selected. He came to visit Zhang Gong, who gave him a bundle of silk to use for food expenses. On his way back to his residence, after not even one or two months, his illness worsened. Using the silk to buy medicine, and when the silk ran out, his illness worsened on its own. Not only did this happen, but it occurred in other places as well. How unfortunate is my fate!

    Governor Pei Youchang of Hangzhou fell gravely ill and ordered the County Magistrate of Qiantang, Xia Rong, to visit him. Xia Rong said, "Sir, there is nothing to worry about; your wife should build merit early to ward off misfortune." Lady Cui said, "What is needed to ward off misfortune?" Xia Rong replied, "Sir, you should marry two concubines to suppress it; if you do not do so within three years, the danger will be even greater." Lady Cui angrily said, "This barbarian is crazy; my son is healthy." Xia Rong stepped back and said, "Lady, if you do not believe me, I dare not speak further; Sir's fate is destined to have three wives; if he does not marry again, it will be a bad omen." Lady Cui said, "It is better to die than to be involved in such matters." That year, Lady Cui suddenly died. Pei Youchang remarried two more women, and Xia Rong's words proved true.

    Prince Guangping killed the traitorous Wei Cui, and General Du Qu led the troops to almost completely eliminate the Wei clan. Even infants in their cradles were strangled to death. Many others were also wrongfully killed. Fuhuizi said, "This is the crime of the traitorous Wei; why should distant relatives suffer? It is like Ran Min killing foreigners with high noses, or Dong Zhuo executing eunuchs without beards, all of which are unjust deaths; such is the will of fate." [18]

    逆韦之变吏部尚书张嘉福河北道存抚使至怀州武涉驿有勅所至处斩之寻有敕矜放使人马上昏睡迟行一驿比至已斩讫岂非天乎
    　　沈君亮见防道事上元年中吏部员外张仁祎延生问曰明公看祎何当迁亮曰台郎坐不暖席何虑不迁俄而祎如厠亮谓诸人曰张员外总十余日活何暇忧官职乎后七日而祎卒
    　　防州司士刘知元摄判司仓大酺时司马杨舜臣谓之曰买肉必须胎肥脆可食余瘦不堪知元乃防取怀孕牛犊及猪羊驴等杀之其胎仍动良久乃絶无何舜臣一奴无病而死心上乃暖七日而苏云见一水犊白额幷子随之见王诉云怀胎五个月扛杀母子须臾又见猪羊驴等皆领子来诉见刘司士答欵引杨司马处分如此居三日而知元卒亡又五日而舜臣死
    　　率更令张文成枭晨鸣于庭树其妻以为不祥连唾之文成云急洒扫吾当改官言未毕贺客已在门矣又一说文成景云二年为鸿胪寺丞帽带及緑袍并被鼠额有神灵逓相诬告京都及郡县被诛戮者数千余家蜀王秀皆坐之隋室既亡其事亦寝矣 [19]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [19]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 逆韋之變 - Refers to the An Lushan Rebellion (755–763), a major uprising during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 吏部尚書 - A high-ranking official in charge of personnel management.
    • 河北道存撫使 - An official tasked with maintaining order and providing relief in the Hebei region.
    • 懷州武涉驛 - A specific location in the Tang Dynasty, where the events took place.
    • 勅所至處斬之 - A direct order to execute someone upon arrival.
    • 尋有勅矜放 - A subsequent order to show mercy and release the person.
    • 使人馬上昏睡 - Describes the messenger falling asleep on horseback.
    • 遲行一驿 - Indicates that the messenger was delayed by one station.
    • 比至已斬訖 - Means that by the time the messenger arrived, the execution had already been carried out.
    • 豈非天乎 - A rhetorical question expressing that this must be the will of heaven.
    • 上元年中 - Refers to the first year of the Shangyuan era (760 CE) during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 吏部員外 - A mid-level official in the Ministry of Personnel.
    • 張仁祎 - A specific official, Zhang Renyi.
    • 延生問曰 - Zhang Renyi asked Shen Junliang a question.
    • 明公看祎何當遷 - Zhang Renyi asked Shen Junliang when he would be promoted.
    • 台郎坐不暖席 - A metaphorical expression meaning that Zhang Renyi had not been in his position long enough to get comfortable.
    • 俄而祎如厠 - Zhang Renyi went to the bathroom.
    • 張員外總十余日活 - Shen Junliang said Zhang Renyi would live for more than ten days.
    • 無暇憂官職乎 - Shen Junliang suggested that Zhang Renyi should not worry about his official position.
    • 后七日而祎卒 - Zhang Renyi died seven days later.
    • 防州司士 - A local official in charge of military affairs.
    • 劉知元 - A specific official, Liu Zhimou.
    • 攝判司倉 - Liu Zhimou temporarily took over the position of managing the granary.
    • 大酺 - A grand feast or celebration.
    • 司马杨舜臣 - A military official named Yang Shunchen.
    • 買肉必須胎肥脆可食 - Liu Zhimou said that meat must be from pregnant animals to be tender and tasty.
    • 余瘦不堪 - Meat from non-pregnant animals is not suitable.
    • 防取懷孕牛犊及猪羊驴等殺之 - Liu Zhimou ordered the killing of pregnant cattle, pigs, sheep, and donkeys.
    • 其胎仍動良久乃絶 - The fetuses continued to move for a long time before dying.
    • 無何舜臣一奴無病而死 - Yang Shunchen's servant died without any apparent illness.
    • 心上乃暖七日而蘇 - The servant's heart warmed up, and he revived after seven days.
    • 見一水犊白額並子隨之 - The servant saw a white-browed water buffalo and its offspring.
    • 見王訴云懷胎五个月扛殺母子 - The water buffalo and its offspring claimed that they were killed because the mother was five months pregnant.
    • 須臾又見猪羊驴等皆領子來訴 - Soon after, the servant saw pigs, sheep, and donkeys with their offspring coming to complain.
    • 見劉司士答欵引楊司马处分如此 - Liu Zhimou admitted to the servant that he had followed Yang Shunchen's orders.
    • 居三日而知元卒亡 - Liu Zhimou died three days later.
    • 又五日而舜臣死 - Yang Shunchen died five days after Liu Zhimou.
    • 率更令 - A local official in charge of timekeeping.
    • 张文成 - A specific official, Zhang Wencheng.
    • 枭晨鸣于庭树 - An owl called out early in the morning from a tree in the courtyard.
    • 其妻以為不祥 - Zhang Wencheng's wife thought it was an omen of bad luck.
    • 連唾之 - She spat at the owl.
    • 文成云急洒扫吾当改官 - Zhang Wencheng said that he should clean the house quickly because he was about to be promoted.
    • 言未畢贺客已在门矣 - Before he finished speaking, guests arrived to congratulate him.
    • 又一说文成景云二年为鸿胪寺丞 - Another account states that in the second year of the Jingyun era (711 CE), Zhang Wencheng was appointed as a vice minister of the Ministry of Rites.
    • 帽带及緑袍并被鼠额有神灵递相诬告 - His hat, belt, and green robe were allegedly possessed by spirits, leading to false accusations.
    • 京都及郡县被诛戮者数千余家 - Thousands of families in the capital and other regions were executed.
    • 蜀王秀皆坐之 - Shu Prince You (Li Xian) was implicated in these accusations.
    • 隋室既亡其事亦寝矣 - With the fall of the Sui Dynasty, the matter was eventually forgotten.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 逆韋之變 - The An Lushan Rebellion, a significant event in Tang history.
    • 吏部尚書 - A high-ranking official responsible for personnel management.
    • 河北道存撫使 - An official responsible for maintaining order and providing relief in the Hebei region.
    • 懷州武涉驛 - A specific location in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 勅所至處斬之 - A direct imperial order to execute someone.
    • 尋有勅矜放 - A subsequent imperial order to show mercy and release the person.
    • 使人馬上昏睡 - Describes a messenger falling asleep on horseback.
    • 遲行一驿 - Indicates a delay of one station.
    • 比至已斬訖 - The execution had already taken place by the time the messenger arrived.
    • 豈非天乎 - A rhetorical question suggesting divine will.
    • 上元年中 - The first year of the Shangyuan era (760 CE).
    • 吏部員外 - A mid-level official in the Ministry of Personnel.
    • 張仁祎 - A specific official, Zhang Renyi.
    • 延生問曰 - Zhang Renyi asked Shen Junliang a question.
    • 明公看祎何當遷 - Zhang Renyi asked Shen Junliang when he would be promoted.
    • 台郎坐不暖席 - A metaphorical expression indicating that Zhang Renyi had not been in his position long enough to get comfortable.
    • 俄而祎如厠 - Zhang Renyi went to the bathroom.
    • 張員外總十余日活 - Shen Junliang predicted that Zhang Renyi would live for more than ten days.
    • 無暇憂官職乎 - Shen Junliang suggested that Zhang Renyi should not worry about his official position.
    • 后七日而祎卒 - Zhang Renyi died seven days later.
    • 防州司士 - A local official in charge of military affairs.
    • 劉知元 - A specific official, Liu Zhimou.
    • 攝判司倉 - Liu Zhimou temporarily took over the position of managing the granary.
    • 大酺 - A grand feast or celebration.
    • 司马杨舜臣 - A military official named Yang Shunchen.
    • 買肉必須胎肥脆可食 - Liu Zhimou said that meat must be from pregnant animals to be tender and tasty.
    • 余瘦不堪 - Meat from non-pregnant animals is not suitable.
    • 防取懷孕牛犊及猪羊驴等殺之 - Liu Zhimou ordered the killing of pregnant cattle, pigs, sheep, and donkeys.
    • 其胎仍動良久乃絶 - The fetuses continued to move for a long time before dying.
    • 無何舜臣一奴無病而死 - Yang Shunchen's servant died without any apparent illness.
    • 心上乃暖七日而蘇 - The servant's heart warmed up, and he revived after seven days.
    • 見一水犊白額並子隨之 - The servant saw a white-browed water buffalo and its offspring.
    • 見王訴云懷胎五个月扛殺母子 - The water buffalo and its offspring claimed that they were killed because the mother was five months pregnant.
    • 須臾又見猪羊驴等皆領子來訴 - Soon after, the servant saw pigs, sheep, and donkeys with their offspring coming to complain.
    • 見劉司士答欵引楊司马处分如此 - Liu Zhimou admitted to the servant that he had followed Yang Shunchen's orders.
    • 居三日而知元卒亡 - Liu Zhimou died three days later.
    • 又五日而舜臣死 - Yang Shunchen died five days after Liu Zhimou.
    • 率更令 - A local official in charge of timekeeping.
    • 张文成 - A specific official, Zhang Wencheng.
    • 枭晨鸣于庭树 - An owl called out early in the morning from a tree in the courtyard.
    • 其妻以為不祥 - Zhang Wencheng's wife thought it was an omen of bad luck.
    • 連唾之 - She spat at the owl.
    • 文成云急洒扫吾当改官 - Zhang Wencheng said that he should clean the house quickly because he was about to be promoted.
    • 言未畢贺客已在门矣 - Before he finished speaking, guests arrived to congratulate him.
    • 又一说文成景云二年为鸿胪寺丞 - Another account states that in the second year of the Jingyun era (711 CE), Zhang Wencheng was appointed as a vice minister of the Ministry of Rites.
    • 帽带及緑袍并被鼠额有神灵递相诬告 - His hat, belt, and green robe were allegedly possessed by spirits, leading to false accusations.
    • 京都及郡县被诛戮者数千余家 - Thousands of families in the capital and other regions were executed.
    • 蜀王秀皆坐之 - Shu Prince You (Li Xian) was implicated in these accusations.
    • 隋室既亡其事亦寝矣 - With the fall of the Sui Dynasty, the matter was eventually forgotten.

    During the An Lushan Rebellion, Zhang Jiafu, the Minister of Personnel, was appointed as the Governor of the Hebei Circuit and tasked with maintaining order and providing relief. Upon arriving at the Wu She Inn in Huizhou, he received an imperial edict ordering his execution upon arrival. However, shortly after, another edict was issued showing mercy and releasing him. The messenger, however, fell asleep on horseback and was delayed by one station. By the time he arrived, Zhang Jiafu had already been executed. This must be the will of heaven.

    Shen Junliang, who witnessed the events, recalled that in the first year of the Shangyuan era, Zhang Renyi, a mid-level official in the Ministry of Personnel, asked him when he would be promoted. Shen Junliang replied that Zhang Renyi had not been in his position long enough to get comfortable, so he should not worry about his promotion. Soon after, Zhang Renyi went to the bathroom, and Shen Junliang told his colleagues that Zhang Renyi would live for more than ten days and should not worry about his official position. However, seven days later, Zhang Renyi died.

    In another incident, Liu Zhimou, a local official in charge of military affairs, was temporarily managing the granary during a grand feast. He ordered the killing of pregnant cattle, pigs, sheep, and donkeys, claiming that their meat was tender and tasty. The fetuses continued to move for a long time before dying. Shortly after, Yang Shunchen's servant died without any apparent illness. The servant's heart warmed up, and he revived after seven days. He claimed to have seen a white-browed water buffalo and its offspring, who said they were killed because the mother was five months pregnant. Soon after, pigs, sheep, and donkeys with their offspring came to complain. Liu Zhimou admitted to the servant that he had followed Yang Shunchen's orders. Three days later, Liu Zhimou died, and five days after that, Yang Shunchen also died.

    In a different story, Zhang Wencheng, a local official in charge of timekeeping, heard an owl call out early in the morning from a tree in his courtyard. His wife thought it was an omen of bad luck and spat at it. Zhang Wencheng said that he should clean the house quickly because he was about to be promoted. Before he finished speaking, guests arrived to congratulate him. Another account states that in the second year of the Jingyun era, Zhang Wencheng was appointed as a vice minister of the Ministry of Rites. His hat, belt, and green robe were allegedly possessed by spirits, leading to false accusations. Thousands of families in the capital and other regions were executed, and Shu Prince You was implicated in these accusations. With the fall of the Sui Dynasty, the matter was eventually forgotten. [19]

    仪凤年中有长星半天出东方三十余日乃灭自是土畨叛匈奴反徐敬业乱白铁余作逆博豫骚动忠万强梁契丹翻营府突厥破赵定麻仁节张遇王孝杰等皆没百万众三十余年兵革不息
    　　调露之后有鸟大如鸠色如乌鹊飞风声千万为队时人谓之鵽雀亦名突厥雀若来突厥必至后至无差
    　　天授中则天好改新字又多忌讳有幽州人寻如意上封云国字中或或乱天象请囗中安武以镇之则天大喜下制即依月余有上封者云武退在囗中与囚字无异不祥之甚则天愕然遽追制改令中为八方字后孝和即位果幽则天于上阳宫
    　　长安二年九月一日太阳蚀尽黙啜贼到幷州至十五日夜月蚀尽贼并退尽俗谚云枣子塞鼻孔悬楼阁却种又云蝉鸣蛁蟟唤黍种餻糜断又谚云春雨甲子赤地千里夏雨甲子垂船入市秋雨甲子禾头生耳冬雨甲子鹊巢下地其年大水
    　　长安四年十月隂雨雪一百余日不见星正月诛逆贼张易之昌宗等则天废 [20]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [20]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 長星半天出東方三十余日乃滅 - Describes a long star appearing in the east for over thirty days before disappearing, which is a metaphor for significant events or omens.
    • 土番叛匈奴反徐敬业亂白鐵余作逆博豫騷動忠萬強梁契丹翻營府突厥破趙定麻仁節張玄遇王孝傑等皆沒百萬衆三十余年兵革不息 - Lists various rebellions, uprisings, and military defeats, emphasizing the continuous warfare and unrest over several decades.
    • 調露之後有鳥大如鳩色如烏鵲飛風聲千萬為隊時人謂之鵽雀亦名突厥雀若來突厥必至後至無差 - Describes a bird seen after the Tiaolu era, which was considered an omen of the arrival of the Turks.
    • 天授中則天好改新字又多忌諱有幽州人尋如意上封云國字中或或亂天象請囗中安武以鎮之則天大喜下制即依月余有上封者云武退在囗中與囚字無異不祥之甚則天愕然遽追制改令中為八方字後孝和即位果幽則天於上陽宮 - Describes Wu Zetian's interest in creating new characters and the subsequent changes in the character '囗' due to superstitions and omens.
    • 長安二年九月一日太陽蝕盡黙啜賊到幷州至十五日夜月蝕盡賊並退盡俗諺云棗子塞鼻孔懸樓閣卻種又云蟬鳴蛁蟟喚黍種餻糜斷又諺云春雨甲子赤地千里夏雨甲子垂船入市秋雨甲子禾頭生耳冬雨甲子鵲巢下地其年大水 - Describes natural phenomena and their associated omens, such as solar and lunar eclipses, and weather-related sayings.
    • 長安四年十月陰雨雪一百余日不見星正月誅逆賊張易之昌宗等則天廢 - Describes a prolonged period of rain and snow followed by the execution of traitors and Wu Zetian's eventual downfall.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 儀鳳年中 - The Yifeng era (676-679) during the Tang Dynasty.
    • 長星 - A celestial phenomenon often interpreted as an omen in ancient Chinese culture.
    • 土番 - A reference to a group or region.
    • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic people.
    • 徐敬业 - A historical figure who led a rebellion.
    • 白鐵余 - A historical figure involved in an uprising.
    • 博豫 - A place or region.
    • 忠萬強梁 - A historical figure or group.
    • 契丹 - The Khitan, a nomadic people.
    • 突厥 - The Turkic people.
    • 趙定麻仁節張玄遇王孝傑 - Historical figures.
    • 調露 - The Tiaolu era (680-681).
    • 鵽雀 - A bird considered an omen.
    • 天授 - The Tianzhu era (690-705).
    • 則天 - Wu Zetian, the Empress Regnant.
    • 幽州 - A region in northern China.
    • 尋如意 - A person who submitted a petition.
    • 長安 - The Chang'an era (702-705).
    • 黙啜 - A historical figure or group.
    • 幷州 - A region in northern China.
    • 孝和 - Emperor Zhongzong of Tang.
    • 上陽宮 - A palace in Luoyang.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • This long sentence lists multiple historical events and figures, requiring careful segmentation to ensure clarity.
    • The sentence describes the bird and its significance, which needs to be broken down for clarity.
    • This complex sentence describes Wu Zetian's actions and the subsequent changes in the character '囗,' requiring careful segmentation.
    • This sentence describes natural phenomena and their associated omens, requiring careful segmentation.
    • This sentence describes a prolonged period of rain and snow followed by the execution of traitors and Wu Zetian's downfall, requiring careful segmentation.

    Proper Nouns

    • 儀鳳 - The Yifeng era (676-679).
    • 長星 - A celestial phenomenon.
    • 土番 - A reference to a group or region.
    • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic people.
    • 徐敬业 - A historical figure who led a rebellion.
    • 白鐵余 - A historical figure involved in an uprising.
    • 博豫 - A place or region.
    • 忠萬強梁 - A historical figure or group.
    • 契丹 - The Khitan, a nomadic people.
    • 突厥 - The Turkic people.
    • 趙定麻仁節張玄遇王孝傑 - Historical figures.
    • 調露 - The Tiaolu era (680-681).
    • 鵽雀 - A bird considered an omen.
    • 天授 - The Tianzhu era (690-705).
    • 則天 - Wu Zetian, the Empress Regnant.
    • 幽州 - A region in northern China.
    • 尋如意 - A person who submitted a petition.
    • 長安 - The Chang'an era (702-705).
    • 黙啜 - A historical figure or group.
    • 幷州 - A region in northern China.
    • 孝和 - Emperor Zhongzong of Tang.
    • 上陽宮 - A palace in Luoyang.

    In the Yifeng era, a long star appeared in the eastern sky, visible for over thirty days before disappearing. From then on, there were rebellions by the Tu Fan, the Xiongnu, and Xu Jingye, as well as uprisings by Bai Tieyu, disturbances in Boyu, and the strong and fierce actions of the Khitans, who turned their camps, the Turks who broke through Zhao Ding, Ma Renshi, Zhang Xuanyu, and Wang Xiaojie, all leading to the loss of a million soldiers. For over thirty years, there was continuous warfare.

    After the Tiaolu era, a bird as large as a dove, colored like a crow, flew with a sound like a thousand winds, forming a group. People called it the "Shuque" or "Turkic Bird," and believed that if it appeared, the Turks would come, and indeed they did, without fail.

    During the Tianzhu era, Wu Zetian had a fondness for creating new characters and was superstitious. A man from Youzhou named Xun Ruyi submitted a petition, suggesting that the character "囗" (which means "country") should have the character "武" (military) placed inside to stabilize it. Wu Zetian was pleased and issued an edict to follow this suggestion. A month later, another petition came, stating that placing "武" inside "囗" made it look like the character for "prison" (囚), which was inauspicious. Wu Zetian was shocked and immediately issued another edict to change the character to "八方" (eight directions). After Emperor Zhongzong ascended the throne, he indeed confined Wu Zetian in the Shangyang Palace.

    In the second year of the Chang'an era, on September 1st, the sun was completely eclipsed, and the Murchech invaders reached Bingzhou. By the night of the 15th, the moon was also eclipsed, and the invaders retreated. Folk sayings went: "When jujubes block the nose, hang the building; when cicadas call, the locusts will come; when the millet is sown, the porridge will be cut; when the wheat sprouts, the magpies will nest on the ground." That year, there was a great flood.

    In the fourth year of the Chang'an era, in October, there was continuous rain and snow for over a hundred days, and the stars were not visible. In January, the traitors Zhang Yizhi and Chang Zhong were executed, and Wu Zetian was deposed. [20]

    幽州都督孙俭之入贼也薛讷与之书曰季月不可入贼大凶也俭曰六月宣王北伐讷何所知有敢言兵出不复者斩出军之日有白虹垂头于军门其夜大星落于营内兵将无敢言者军行后幽州界内鵶鸟防鸢等并失皆随军去经二旬而军没乌鸢食其肉焉
    　　延和初七日太白昼见经天其月太上皇逊帝位此易主之应也至八月九日太白仍昼见改元先天至二月七日太上皇废诛中书令萧至忠侍中岑羲流崔湜寻诛之
    　　开元二年五月二十九日夜大流星如瓮或如盆大者贯北斗并西北小者随之无数天星尽揺至晓乃止七月襄王崩諡帝十月土蕃入陇右掠羊马杀伤无数其年六月大风拔树发屋长安街中树连根出者十七八长安城初建隋将作大匠高颎所植槐树殆三百余年至是拔出终南山竹开花结子绵亘山谷大小如麦其嵗大饥其竹并枯死岭南亦然人取而食之醴泉雨麺如米颗人可食之后汉裴楷云国中竹栢枯者不出三年主当之人家竹结实枯死者家长当之终南竹花枯死者开元四年而太上皇崩 [21]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [21]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 季月不可入贼大凶也 - It is extremely inauspicious to enter the enemy's territory in the last month of the year.
    • 大流星如瓮或如盆大者贯北斗并西北小者随之无数天星尽摇至晓乃止 - A large meteor shower, with large meteors resembling jars or basins passing through the Big Dipper and smaller ones following in great numbers, causing all stars to tremble until dawn.
    • 太上皇逊帝位 - The emperor abdicated to become the retired emperor.
    • 大风拔树发屋长安街中树连根出者十七八 - A strong wind uprooted trees and blew off roofs, with about seventeen out of twenty trees in Chang'an being uprooted.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 幽州都督 - A high-ranking military official in the Tang Dynasty.
    • 太白昼见 - The visibility of Venus in the daytime, often considered an ominous sign.
    • 太上皇 - A retired emperor who has abdicated but retains some authority.
    • 中书令 - A high-ranking official in the central government.
    • 侍中 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court.
    • 土蕃 - The Tibetan Empire, a powerful state that frequently invaded Chinese territories.
    • 长安 - The capital city of the Tang Dynasty.
    • 终南山 - A mountain range near Chang'an, known for its natural beauty and historical significance.
    • 竹开花结子 - A rare and often ominous natural phenomenon where bamboo flowers and produces seeds, followed by a period of famine.

    Proper Nouns

    • 孙俭 - A historical figure, the Youzhou Governor.
    • 薛讷 - A historical figure, a general who wrote to Sun Jian.
    • 萧至忠 - A historical figure, the Chancellor of the Central Secretariat.
    • 岑羲 - A historical figure, the Vice-Chancellor.
    • 崔湜 - A historical figure, an official who was exiled and later executed.
    • 襄王 - The Prince of Xiang.
    • 高颎 - A historical figure, the Chief Architect of the Sui Dynasty.
    • 裴楷 - A historical figure, a scholar from the Eastern Han Dynasty.

    When the Youzhou Governor Sun Jian fell into enemy hands, General Xue Ne wrote to him, saying, "It is extremely inauspicious to enter the enemy's territory in the last month of the year." Sun Jian replied, "In the sixth month, Duke Xuan led a northern expedition. What do you know? Anyone who dares to say that the army will not return will be executed." On the day the army set out, a white rainbow hung from the entrance of the camp, and that night a large star fell within the camp. Soldiers and generals dared not speak up. After the army left, all ravens, kites, and other birds within Youzhou disappeared and followed the army. After twenty days, the army was annihilated, and the birds ate their flesh.

    In the first month of the Yanyuan era, on the seventh day, Venus was visible in the daytime across the sky. That month, the emperor abdicated to become the retired emperor, an omen of a change in leadership. By the ninth day of the eighth month, Venus was still visible in the daytime, and the era name was changed to Tianxian. On the seventh day of the second month, the retired emperor had the Chancellor of the Central Secretariat, Xiao Zhizhong, and the Vice-Chancellor, Cen Xi, executed, and the official Cui Shi exiled before eventually executing him.

    In the second year of the Kaiyuan era, on the night of the twenty-ninth day of the fifth month, a large meteor shower occurred, with large meteors resembling jars or basins passing through the Big Dipper and smaller ones following in great numbers, causing all stars to tremble until dawn. In the seventh month, the Prince of Xiang died and was posthumously titled. In October, the Tibetans invaded Longyou, stealing livestock and causing numerous casualties. In June of that year, a strong wind uprooted trees and blew off roofs, with about seventeen out of twenty trees in Chang'an being uprooted. The construction of Chang'an City began with the planting of over three hundred locust trees by Gao Jing, the Chief Architect of the Sui Dynasty, which were uprooted during the storm. In the Zhongnan Mountains, bamboo bloomed and produced seeds, covering the mountains like wheat. The following year was a great famine, with bamboo dying throughout the region. The same phenomenon occurred in Lingnan, where people ate the bamboo seeds. In Lique, a rain of noodles like rice grains fell, which people could eat. Later, Pei Kai of the Eastern Han Dynasty said that when bamboo and cypress trees in the country wither, the owner will die within three years, and if a bamboo tree in a household withers and produces fruit, the head of the household will die. In the fourth year of the Kaiyuan era, the retired emperor died. [21]

    开元五年洪潭二州复有火灾昼日人见火精赤炖炖所诣即火起东晋时王为吴郡太守亦有此灾挞部人将为不慎后坐防事见一物赤如信幡飞向人家舎上俄而火起方知变不复由人遭人家遂免刺罚
    　　开元八年契丹叛闗中兵救营府至渑池缺门营于谷水侧夜半水涨漂二万余人惟有人夜摴蒱不睡据高获免村店并没尽上阳宫中水溢宫人死者十七八其年京兴道坊一夜陷为池没五百家初邓州三鵶口见二小儿以水相泼须防有大蛇十围已上张口向天人或有斫射者俄而云雨晦防雨水漂二百家小儿及蛇不知所在 [22]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [22]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 开元五年 - The fifth year of Kaiyuan, the reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang (717 AD).
    • 洪潭二州复有火灾 - Refers to the recurrence of fires in two states (洪州 and 潭州) during the fifth year of Kaiyuan.
    • 昼日人见火精赤炖炖所诣即火起 - Describes the phenomenon where people saw red, glowing embers during the day, which then led to the outbreak of fire wherever they appeared.
    • 东晋时王弘为吴郡太守亦有此灾 - Refers to an incident in the Eastern Jin dynasty when Wang Hong, the governor of Wu Prefecture, also experienced similar disasters.
    • 弘挞部人将为不慎 - Wang Hong punished his subordinates for being careless.
    • 后坐防事见一物赤如信幡飞向人家舍上俄而火起方知变不复由人遭火人家遂免刺罚 - Later, due to an incident involving a red object resembling a flag flying towards a house, a fire broke out, revealing that the disaster was not caused by human error. Those affected by the fire were thus exempt from punishment.
    • 开元八年契丹叛关中兵救营府至渑池缺门营于谷水侧夜半水涨漂二万余人惟有人夜摴蒱不睡据高获免村店并没尽上阳宫中水溢宫人死者十七八其年京兴道坊一夜陷为池没五百家初邓州三鵶口见二小儿以水相泼须防有大蛇十围已上张口向天人或有斫射者俄而云雨晦防雨水漂二百家小儿及蛇不知所在 - Describes a rebellion by the Khitan people in the eighth year of Kaiyuan. Chinese troops sent to save the situation camped near the Yellow River, where a sudden flood swept away over 20,000 people. Only those who stayed awake playing dice and took high ground were saved. Villages and shops were destroyed, and in the Shangyang Palace, nearly 18 out of 20 palace women died. In the same year, a section of the capital city sank into a lake, submerging 500 households. Initially, in Dengzhou, three crows' mouths saw two children splashing water, followed by the appearance of a large snake over ten encirclements wide, opening its mouth to the sky. Some people tried to shoot it, and soon after, a heavy rainstorm caused more flooding, drowning 200 households and the children and snake, whose whereabouts are unknown.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 开元 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
    • 洪州 - A state in southern China during the Tang dynasty.
    • 潭州 - A state in southern China during the Tang dynasty.
    • 东晋 - The Eastern Jin dynasty (317–420 AD).
    • 王弘 - A historical figure who served as the governor of Wu Prefecture during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 契丹 - An ethnic group that later formed the Liao dynasty.
    • 关中 - The region around Chang'an (modern Xi'an), the capital of the Tang dynasty.
    • 渑池 - A place name near the Yellow River.
    • 缺门 - A place name near the Yellow River.
    • 谷水侧 - The side of the Yellow River.
    • 上阳宫 - A palace in the Tang capital.
    • 京兴道坊 - A district in the Tang capital.
    • 邓州 - A state in central China during the Tang dynasty.
    • 三鵶口 - A place name in Dengzhou.
    • 信幡 - A flag or banner used in religious or ceremonial contexts.

    Proper Nouns

    • 开元 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
    • 洪州 - A state in southern China during the Tang dynasty.
    • 潭州 - A state in southern China during the Tang dynasty.
    • 东晋 - The Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 王弘 - A historical figure who served as the governor of Wu Prefecture during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 契丹 - An ethnic group that later formed the Liao dynasty.
    • 关中 - The region around Chang'an (modern Xi'an), the capital of the Tang dynasty.
    • 渑池 - A place name near the Yellow River.
    • 缺门 - A place name near the Yellow River.
    • 谷水侧 - The side of the Yellow River.
    • 上阳宫 - A palace in the Tang capital.
    • 京兴道坊 - A district in the Tang capital.
    • 邓州 - A state in central China during the Tang dynasty.
    • 三鵶口 - A place name in Dengzhou.
    • 信幡 - A flag or banner used in religious or ceremonial contexts.

    During the fifth year of Kaiyuan, the states of Hongzhou and Tanzhou experienced another series of fires. During the day, people saw red, glowing embers, and wherever they appeared, fires broke out. In the Eastern Jin dynasty, when Wang Hong served as the governor of Wu Prefecture, he also encountered a similar disaster. He punished his subordinates for being careless. Later, due to an incident involving a red object resembling a flag flying towards a house, a fire broke out, revealing that the disaster was not caused by human error. Those affected by the fire were thus exempt from punishment.

    In the eighth year of Kaiyuan, the Khitan people rebelled, and Chinese troops were sent to save the situation. They camped near the Yellow River, where a sudden flood swept away over 20,000 people. Only those who stayed awake playing dice and took high ground were saved. Villages and shops were destroyed, and in the Shangyang Palace, nearly 18 out of 20 palace women died. In the same year, a section of the capital city sank into a lake, submerging 500 households. Initially, in Dengzhou, three crows' mouths saw two children splashing water, followed by the appearance of a large snake over ten encirclements wide, opening its mouth to the sky. Some people tried to shoot it, and soon after, a heavy rainstorm caused more flooding, drowning 200 households and the children and snake, whose whereabouts are unknown. [22]

    洛阳县令宋之逊性好唱歌出为连州参军刺史陈希古者庸人也令之逊教婢歌每日端笏立于庭中而唱其婢隔窻从而和之闻者无不大笑
    　　朝野佥载卷一
    　　钦定四库全书
    　　朝野佥载卷二
    　　唐　张鷟　撰
    　　北齐南阳王入朝上问何以为乐王曰致蝎最乐遂收蝎一宿得五防置大浴斛中一人脱衣而入被蝎螫死宛转号呌苦痛不可言食顷而死帝与王看之
    　　隋末荒乱狂贼朱粲起于襄邓间嵗饥米斛万钱亦无得处人民相食粲乃驱男女小大仰一大铜钟可二伯石煮人肉以餧贼生灵殱于此矣
    　　周恩州刺史陈承亲岭南大首领也专使子弟兵刼江有一县令从安南来承亲凭买二婢令有难色承亲每日重设邀屈甚殷勤送别江亭即遣子弟兵寻复劫杀尽取财物将其妻女至州妻叩头求作婢不许亦缢杀之取其女前后官人家过亲礼遇厚者必随后刼杀无人得免 [23]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [23]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 性好唱歌 - Indicates a personal preference or inclination, 'has a fondness for singing'
    • 出为连州参军 - Describes a change in position, 'was appointed to the position of military officer in Lianzhou'
    • 刺史陈希古者庸人也 - A judgmental statement about a person's character, 'The prefect Chen Xigǔ was an ordinary person'
    • 令之逊教婢歌 - Describes an action taken by the person, 'Song Zhixun ordered the maid to sing'
    • 每日端笏立于庭中 - Describes a formal or respectful posture, 'Every day he held a jade tablet respectfully in the courtyard'
    • 闻者无不大笑 - Indicates a strong reaction from those who heard the event, 'Those who heard it all laughed loudly'
    • 隋末荒乱 - Sets the historical context, 'At the end of the Sui Dynasty, during a time of chaos'
    • 岭南大首领 - Refers to a prominent figure in a specific region, 'Major leader in the south of the Five Ridges'
    • 煮人肉以餧贼 - Describes a horrific act, 'Cooked human flesh to feed the bandits'
    • 专使子弟兵刼江 - Describes a series of events, 'Specially sent his sons and soldiers to rob the river'
    • 凭买二婢 - Describes an action, 'Relying on him to buy two maids'
    • 甚殷勤送别江亭 - Describes a courteous act, 'Sent him off at the riverside pavilion with great courtesy'
    • 尽取财物 - Describes an action, 'Took all the wealth'
    • 亦缢杀之 - Describes an action, 'Also strangled her to death'
    • 前后官人家过亲礼遇厚者 - Describes a recurring pattern, 'Before and after, officials' families who were closely related and treated with great courtesy'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 洛阳县令宋之逊 - Refers to a local official in a specific historical context, 'The county magistrate of Luoyang, Song Zhixun'
    • 连州参军 - Refers to a specific administrative and military role, 'Military officer in Lianzhou'
    • 朝野佥载卷一 - Provides context for the source of the text, 'Volume One of Cháoyě Qiānzǎi'
    • 钦定四库全书 - Indicates the official and authoritative nature of the text, 'Imperial Library'
    • 北齐南阳王 - Refers to a specific historical event involving a nobleman, 'Nobleman of the Northern Qi Dynasty'
    • 隋末荒乱 - Sets the historical context, 'At the end of the Sui Dynasty, during a time of chaos'
    • 岭南大首领 - Refers to a prominent figure in a specific region, 'Major leader in the south of the Five Ridges'
    • 安南 - Refers to the southern part of China or Vietnam, 'Annam'

    Proper Nouns

    • 洛阳县令宋之逊 - Refers to a specific historical figure, 'Song Zhixun, the county magistrate of Luoyang'
    • 连州参军 - Refers to a specific administrative and military role, 'Military officer in Lianzhou'
    • 刺史陈希古 - Refers to a specific historical figure, 'Chen Xigǔ, the prefect'
    • 北齐南阳王 - Refers to a specific historical figure, 'Nobleman of the Northern Qi Dynasty'
    • 隋末荒乱狂贼朱粲 - Refers to a specific historical figure, 'Bandit Zhu Càn at the end of the Sui Dynasty'
    • 周恩州刺史陈承亲 - Refers to a specific historical figure, 'Zhou En, the prefect of Zhouzhou'
    • 岭南大首领 - Refers to a specific historical figure, 'Major leader in the south of the Five Ridges'
    • 安南 - Refers to a specific historical region, 'Annam'

    The County Magistrate of Luoyang, Song Zhixun, was fond of singing and was appointed to the position of military officer in Lianzhou. The prefect Chen Xigǔ was an ordinary person. Song Zhixun ordered the maid to sing, and every day he held a jade tablet respectfully in the courtyard, singing, while the maid followed through the window and sang along. Those who heard it all laughed loudly.

    Volume One of Chronicles of the Court and the World
    The Imperial Library, Four Treasuries

    Volume Two of Chronicles of the Court and the World
    By Zhang Zhuo of the Tang Dynasty

    During the Northern Qi period, the Prince of Nanyang entered the capital. The emperor asked what he found most enjoyable. The prince replied, "The most enjoyable thing is to catch scorpions." They were then caught and placed in a large bathing tub, which could hold two hundred stones. One person removed his clothes and entered the tub, only to be stung to death by the scorpions. He writhed and cried out in unbearable pain for a short time before dying. The emperor and the prince watched him die.

    At the end of the Sui Dynasty, during a time of chaos, the bandit Zhu Càn rose in the area between Xiang and Dang. In a year of famine, a measure of rice cost ten thousand coins, and there was no way to get it. People ate each other. Zhu Càn then drove men and women of all ages to a large bronze bell, which could hold two hundred stones, cooked human flesh to feed the bandits, and destroyed the lives of the people there.

    Zhou En, the prefect of Zhouzhou, was a major leader in the south of the Five Ridges. He specially sent his sons and soldiers to rob the river. A county magistrate from Annan came, and Zhou En relied on him to buy two maids. The magistrate had a difficult expression. Zhou En set up a feast every day, inviting him with great courtesy, and sent him off at the riverside pavilion. Immediately after, he sent his sons and soldiers to rob and kill, taking all the wealth, bringing his wife and daughters to the prefecture. The wife knelt and begged to be a maid, but he did not allow it, and also strangled her to death, taking her daughter. Before and after, officials' families who were closely related and treated with great courtesy were always robbed and killed, and no one could escape. [23]

    周杭州临安尉薛震好食人肉有债主及奴诣临安于客舎遂饮之醉杀而脔之以水银和煎幷骨销尽之后又欲食其妇妇觉而遁之县令诘具得其情申州録事奏奉敕杖一百而死
    　　周岭南首领陈元光设客令一袍袴行酒光怒令曳出遂杀之须防烂煮以食客后呈其二手客惧攫喉而吐
    　　周瀛州刺史独孤庄酷虐有贼问不承庄引前曰若健儿一一具吐放汝遂还巾带贼并吐之诸官以为必放顷庄曰将我作具来乃一铁钩长丈余甚铦利以绳挂于树间谓贼曰汝不闻健儿钩下死今以胲钩之遣壮士掣其绳则钩出于脑矣谓司法曰此法何似答曰吊民伐罪深得其宜庄大笑后庄左降施州刺史染病惟忆人肉部下有奴婢死者遣人割肋下肉食之嵗余卒
    　　周推事使索元礼时人号为索使讯囚作铁笼头【呼角反】其头仍加揳焉多至脑裂髓出又为凤晒翅狝猴鑚火等以椽闗手足而转之并斫骨至碎又悬囚于梁下以石缒头其酷法如此元礼外域人薛师之假父后坐贿流死岭南 [24]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [24]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 好食人肉 - Literal meaning 'likes to eat human flesh,' a euphemism for cannibalism.
    • 债主及奴诣临安于客舎遂饮之醉杀而脔之以水银和煎幷骨销尽 - Describes a violent sequence: creditor and slave arrived at Lin'an in an inn, drank with the victim, got him drunk, killed him, chopped him into pieces, cooked with mercury, and melted down to the bones.
    • 周岭南首领陈元光设客令一袍袴行酒光怒令曳出遂杀之须防烂煮以食客后呈其二手客惧攫喉而吐 - During the Zhou dynasty, the leader of Lingnan, Chen Yuanguang, set up a banquet and ordered a servant to serve wine, Chen became angry, ordered him to be dragged out and killed, his body was boiled until soft and served to guests, later his hands were shown, guests were terrified and choked and vomited.
